event: admitted to the hospital for atypical chest pain
no evidence of coronary disease at coronary angiography
developed a superficial phlebitis of the left forearm
empirical antimicrobial therapy with amoxicillin and clavulanic acid
lumbar puncture revealed purulent cerebrospinal fluid
referred to third hospital on day 14
MRI brain and spinal cord performed
signal abnormalities extended to medulla oblongata
no significant changes in cerebral hemispheres
peripheral activities P9 and N13 complex normal
able to respond by vertical eye movements
blood cultures positive for S. aureus
endocarditis ruled out by transesophageal echocardiography
signal hyperintensity extended to cerebellar tonsils
signal hyperintensity extended to thoracic spinal cord
T1-weighted imaging hypointensity of grey matter
brain stem reflexes absent on day 21
persisting wave I upon left ear stimulation
decided to withdraw intensive care therapy
